title: Account and Sign-In
excerpt: Create a Qesto account, sign in with magic link or password, and manage personal settings.
content: # Account and Sign-In

title: Account and Sign-In: Creating an Account
excerpt: Hosts need a Qesto account to create and run sessions.
content:
## Creating an Account

Hosts need a Qesto account to create and run sessions. Participants usually do **not** need an account — they join via link or code.

### Sign up

1. Go to [qesto.cc](https://qesto.cc) and click **Sign up**
2. Enter your email and name, and choose a password (minimum 8 characters)
3. You are logged in and taken to the dashboard

Alternatively, use **Magic link** on the login page: enter your email and click the link we send you — no password required.

title: Account and Sign-In: Signing In
excerpt: Qesto supports two sign-in methods:
content:
## Signing In

Qesto supports two sign-in methods:

### Magic link (recommended)

1. Open the login page → **Magic link** tab
2. Enter your email
3. Check your inbox for an email from Qesto
4. Click the link — you are signed in automatically

Links expire after a short time for security. Request a new one if yours expired.

### Email and password

1. Open the login page → **Log in** tab
2. Enter email and password
3. Click **Log in**

Forgot your password? Use **Reset password** on the login tab. We email you a reset link.

title: Account and Sign-In: I Didn't Receive the Magic Link
excerpt: Create a Qesto account, sign in with magic link or password, and manage personal settings.
content:
## I Didn't Receive the Magic Link

Try these steps:

1. **Check spam/junk** — automated emails sometimes land there
2. **Wait 2–3 minutes** — delivery can be delayed
3. **Confirm the email address** — typos are the most common cause
4. **Try again** — request a fresh link (old links expire)
5. **Use password login** if you already set a password

Still stuck? Email support@qesto.cc with the address you used. We respond within 2 hours.

title: Account and Sign-In: Personal Settings
excerpt: Open Settings from the sidebar footer (/settings):
content:
## Personal Settings

Open **Settings** from the sidebar footer (`/settings`):

- **Email** — view the address on your account
- **Language** — choose EN, NL, ES, DE, or FR (applies across the app)
- **List density** — compact or comfortable dashboard lists
- **Billing** — upgrade, manage payment method, download invoices (Signal+)

Settings is for **your account**. Team workspace settings (members, SAML) live under **Teams → [team name] → Settings**.

title: Account and Sign-In: Signing Out
excerpt: Click your profile menu → Sign out.
content:
## Signing Out

Click your profile menu → **Sign out**. Your sessions and data remain saved.

title: Account and Sign-In: Account vs Participant
excerpt: Create a Qesto account, sign in with magic link or password, and manage personal settings.
content:
## Account vs Participant

| Role | Account needed? |
|------|-----------------|
| Host (create/run sessions) | Yes |
| Team member (collaborate) | Yes |
| Participant (vote in a session) | No — join via link or code |

title: Account and Sign-In: Questions?
excerpt: A: Contact support@qesto.cc — we can update it for you.
content:
## Questions?

**Q: Can I use the same email on multiple devices?**
A: Yes. Sign in on any supported browser; your sessions sync to your account.

**Q: Can I change my email address?**
A: Contact support@qesto.cc — we can update it for you.

**Q: How do I delete my account?**
A: Email support@qesto.cc from your account email. We process deletion requests under our privacy policy (typically within 30 days).

title: Pricing and Plans

# Pricing and Plans

Qesto offers three subscription plans. Official names and limits are published at [qesto.cc/pricing](https://qesto.cc/pricing).

title: Pricing and Plans: Pulse
excerpt: Perfect for trying Qesto:
content:
## Pulse

**€0 / host / month**

Perfect for trying Qesto:

- Up to 5 new sessions per month
- Up to 50 participants per session
- All question types
- 30-day retention
- Full and cohort anonymity modes
- Email support

**Great for:** Small teams, testing, getting started

title: Pricing and Plans: Signal
excerpt: For facilitators running recurring rooms:
content:
## Signal

**€24 / host / month billed annually** (€29 month-to-month)

For facilitators running recurring rooms:

- Everything in Pulse, plus:
- Up to 50 new sessions per month
- Up to 500 participants per session
- 365-day retention
- CSV export of results
- Identified mode + consent logs
- Custom branding
- Priority email support

**Great for:** Departments, mid-size teams, regular facilitators

title: Pricing and Plans: Chorus
excerpt: For HR, events, and compliance-heavy rollouts:
content:
## Chorus

**Custom annual contract** — talk to us

For HR, events, and compliance-heavy rollouts:

- Everything in Signal, plus:
- Up to 500 new sessions per month
- Up to 5,000 participants per session
- AI-powered insights and evidence-anchored clusters
- SAML SSO and advanced auth
- Townhall Q&A board
- Cross-session intelligence
- Embed widgets, live captions, and webhooks
- Dedicated onboarding + SLA

**Great for:** Large organizations, multiple workspaces, complex workflows

title: Pricing and Plans: Refund Policy
excerpt: last survey, email us within 14 days of your first billing and we'll refund the full
content:
## Refund Policy

- **First-session guarantee**: If your first session doesn't beat the response rate of your
  last survey, email us within **14 days of your first billing** and we'll refund the full
  quarter.
- **Monthly plans**: Cancel anytime. Cancellation takes effect immediately and there's no
  refund for the current month.
- **Annual plans**: Cancellation takes effect at your renewal date (you keep access until then).
- **Disputes**: Contact support@qesto.cc

The published terms always live at [qesto.cc/pricing](https://qesto.cc/pricing) — if anything
here ever differs, the pricing page wins.

title: Pricing and Plans: Failed Payments
excerpt: If your card declines, you don't lose access right away.
content:
## Failed Payments

If your card declines, you don't lose access right away. Qesto uses Stripe's standard retry
(dunning) process:

1. We'll notify you by email.
2. Your subscription stays active (marked *past due*) while Stripe automatically retries the
   charge over its retry window.
3. Update your card and the next retry succeeds — nothing else changes.
4. Only if every retry fails does the subscription end, at which point your account reverts to
   the free **Pulse** tier. Your sessions and data are preserved; new sessions follow Pulse
   limits until you resubscribe.

**To fix:** Settings → Billing → Update payment method.

title: Pricing and Plans: Tax
excerpt: A: Yes — registered nonprofits and accredited educational institutions get Chorus at 40% off.
content:
## Tax

- US: Sales tax calculated based on your state
- EU: VAT applied (reverse charge for B2B)
- International: VAT/GST varies by country
- Non-profit: Email support for discount (Chorus at 40% off for registered nonprofits and accredited educational institutions)

**Q: Do you offer nonprofit or education discounts?**
A: Yes — registered nonprofits and accredited educational institutions get Chorus at 40% off. Apply via the pricing page or email support@qesto.cc.

**Q: Can I get a VAT invoice for my company?**
A: Yes. EU B2B customers may use reverse charge. Invoices include tax details — download from Settings → Billing → Invoices.

**Q: What happens to my sessions if payment fails?**
A: After 5 days with a declined card, your plan downgrades to Pulse. Sessions and data are preserved; new sessions are limited to Pulse quotas.

**Q: Can I switch from monthly to annual billing?**
A: Yes. Settings → Billing → Change Plan → select annual. Prorated credit applies to the switch.

title: Pricing and Plans: Upgrading and Downgrading Your Plan
excerpt: Want more features or participants?
content:
## Upgrading and Downgrading Your Plan

### Upgrading Your Plan

Want more features or participants?

#### Steps

1. Go to Settings → Billing
2. Click "Change Plan"
3. Select your new plan (Signal or Chorus)
4. Choose billing period (Monthly or Annual)
5. Review the charge
6. Click "Upgrade"

#### What Happens

- **Immediate activation**: New features available right away
- **Prorated charge**: You're charged the difference for the remainder of the current billing cycle
- **Next renewal**: Full price for new plan

**Example**: Upgrade from Pulse to Signal mid-month
- Pulse: €0
- Signal: €29/month
- Days remaining: 15
- Charge: ~€14.50 (prorated)
- Next month: Full €29 charge

### Downgrading Your Plan

Need fewer features?

#### Steps

1. Settings → Billing → "Change Plan"
2. Select your new plan (Pulse or Signal)
3. Review the change
4. Click "Downgrade"

#### What Happens

- **Prorated credit**: Unused portion credited to your account
- **Features limited**: Access to downgraded plan features immediately
- **Active sessions**: Unaffected (you keep current sessions)
- **Next renewal**: New plan price

**Example**: Downgrade from Signal to Pulse mid-month
- Signal: €29/month
- Days remaining: 15
- Credit: ~€14.50 (prorated)
- Next month: Pulse (€0)

title: Pricing and Plans: Timing Matters
excerpt: Understand Qesto's three subscription plans — Pulse, Signal, and Chorus — plus payment, refunds, failed payments, and plan management.
content:
## Timing Matters

**Best time to change plans**: Right after your renewal date (less proration)

**Can I change plans multiple times?** Yes, but note that frequent changes may incur small prorations.

title: Pricing and Plans: What About My Data?

## What About My Data?

Downgrading doesn't delete your data:

- **Sessions**: All sessions preserved
- **Response data**: Stays in your account
- **Limits apply**: Pulse has lower session and participant limits per new session
- **Export first**: If worried, export CSV from Signal before downgrading

title: Pricing and Plans: Annual Plans

## Annual Plans

Special rules for annual billing:

- **Cancellation**: Takes effect at your renewal date — you keep access for the rest of the
  term you've paid for.
- **First-session guarantee**: The 14-day first-session refund (see Refund Policy above) still
  applies to your first billing.
- **Upgrade**: Always prorated, new plan takes effect immediately.

title: Enterprise Data Controls — Residency, Federation, and Sovereign Tier
excerpt: Enterprise controls for where your data lives and how organizations connect — data residency pinning, cross-org federation, and the Sovereign+ tier with verifiable audit exports.
content:
# Enterprise Data Controls

For organizations with compliance, data-sovereignty, or multi-org requirements, Qesto offers
controls beyond the standard plans. These are **Chorus / enterprise** capabilities and are
typically configured by a team owner or administrator. For the full legal and security detail,
your security team can also refer to Qesto's compliance documentation; this page is the
plain-language overview.

title: Enterprise Data Controls — Residency, Federation, and Sovereign Tier: Data Residency
excerpt: Pin a team's data to a specific region so that session data stays where your policy requires.
content:
## Data Residency

Pin a team's data to a specific region so that session data stays where your policy requires.

- An owner sets the team's **residency region**.
- Once pinned, Qesto enforces that the team's data is stored and processed in that region.
- Useful for meeting EU (or other regional) data-handling requirements.

title: Enterprise Data Controls — Residency, Federation, and Sovereign Tier: Federation (cross-org trust)
excerpt: Federation lets separate Qesto organizations form trust links so they can collaborate —
content:
## Federation (cross-org trust)

Federation lets separate Qesto organizations form **trust links** so they can collaborate —
for example, sharing a session library or running joint sessions — without merging into one
tenant. Federation is **consent-based**: both organizations must opt in to the link, and each
side keeps control of its own data and can revoke the link.

title: Enterprise Data Controls — Residency, Federation, and Sovereign Tier: Sovereign+ Tier
excerpt: The Sovereign+ tier is for the strictest data-sovereignty needs.
content:
## Sovereign+ Tier

The **Sovereign+** tier is for the strictest data-sovereignty needs. On top of residency it
adds:

- **Per-region edge residency** — data stays within a designated sovereign boundary.
- **Compliance posture matrix** — a view of how the tenant maps to its compliance controls.
- **Verifiable audit export** — a signed export of audit activity that an auditor can
  independently verify hasn't been tampered with.
- **Federation eligibility controls** — decide whether (and with whom) a sovereign tenant may
  federate.

title: Enterprise Data Controls — Residency, Federation, and Sovereign Tier: Getting these features
excerpt: Residency, Federation, and Sovereign+ are enterprise capabilities on the Chorus plan and
content:
## Getting these features

Residency, Federation, and Sovereign+ are enterprise capabilities on the **Chorus** plan and
are enabled as part of your contract and onboarding. Talk to us via
[qesto.cc/pricing](https://qesto.cc/pricing) → *Book a walkthrough*, or contact
support@qesto.cc. For data-rights and retention questions, see
[Privacy, GDPR, and Data Rights](privacy-gdpr.md).

title: Frequently Asked Questions
excerpt: Answers to common questions about using Qesto, limits, compatibility, and more.
content: # Frequently Asked Questions

title: Frequently Asked Questions: General
excerpt: A: Qesto works on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge (desktop and mobile).
content:
## General

**Q: What browser do I need?**
A: Qesto works on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge (desktop and mobile). We recommend the latest version for best compatibility.

**Q: Can I use Qesto on mobile?**
A: Yes! Qesto is fully responsive. Participants can join and vote from any device.

**Q: How many people can join a session?**
A: Pulse: 50 participants per session. Signal: 500. Chorus: 5,000. You can always upgrade.

**Q: Is there a session time limit?**
A: No! Sessions can run as long as you need. However, we recommend closing after 2-3 hours for performance.

**Q: Can I edit questions after starting?**
A: No, questions are locked once the session goes live. You can add new questions, but can't edit existing ones.

**Q: Do I need a video call platform to use Qesto?**
A: No, Qesto is standalone. But it pairs great with Zoom, Teams, or Google Meet for facilitating discussions.

title: Frequently Asked Questions: Data & Privacy
excerpt: A: Yes, all data is encrypted in transit (HTTPS) and at rest.
content:
## Data & Privacy

**Q: Is my data encrypted?**
A: Yes, all data is encrypted in transit (HTTPS) and at rest.

**Q: Can I export my results?**
A: Yes! Download results as CSV from any closed session (Signal+ plans).

**Q: Do you store data outside the US?**
A: Qesto is hosted on Cloudflare's global edge network (data centers worldwide). All data complies with GDPR and CCPA.

**Q: How long do you keep my data?**
A: Retention depends on your plan — Pulse targets 30 days, Signal 365 days, and Chorus offers configurable retention from 7 days up to 7 years. Sessions persist until you delete them; automatic age-based purges are not yet enforced for ordinary session data. See the Data Retention help article for details.

title: Frequently Asked Questions: Usage
excerpt: A: Yes, the Pulse plan includes 5 sessions per month with up to 50 participants and all 9 question types.
content:
## Usage

**Q: Can I use Qesto for free?**
A: Yes, the Pulse plan includes 5 sessions per month with up to 50 participants and all 9 question types.

**Q: What's included in each plan?**
A: See our pricing page for a detailed comparison.

**Q: Can I cancel anytime?**
A: Yes, no lock-in contracts. You can downgrade or cancel anytime. Monthly cancellations take effect immediately (no refund for the current month); annual cancellations take effect at your renewal date.

**Q: Can I get a refund?**
A: Yes, under our first-session guarantee: if your first session doesn't beat the response rate of your last survey, email support@qesto.cc within 14 days of your first billing and we'll refund the full quarter. See [Pricing and Plans](billing.md) for full terms.

**Q: Do participants need an account?**
A: No. Participants join via link or code without signing up. Only hosts and team collaborators need accounts.

**Q: How do I join a session?**
A: Open the link your host shared, or go to qesto.cc/join and enter the short code. Enter a display name and wait for the host to start.

**Q: Can I change my vote after submitting?**
A: Usually no — the default is one vote per question. Hosts can set **Reactions** policy to allow updates while a question is open, or use multi-select/upvote types for multiple choices.

**Q: What languages does Qesto support?**
A: English, Dutch, Spanish, German, and French. Change language in Settings or the header language switcher.

**Q: How do I duplicate a session?**
A: Dashboard → select a session → **Duplicate**. A new DRAFT copy is created with the same questions.

**Q: What's the difference between DRAFT, LIVE, and CLOSED?**
A: DRAFT = setup only (not joinable). LIVE = participants can vote. CLOSED = interaction ended; results and insights available.

**Q: Can I use templates?**
A: Yes. Dashboard → Templates → pick a Qesto or team template → **Use template** → customize in the wizard.

**Q: How does AI question generation work?**
A: In the session wizard, describe your goal and AI suggests questions. Review and edit before saving. You consent to AI processing when prompted.

**Q: When are AI insights available?**
A: After a session closes, on the Chorus plan. You need open-ended responses — polls alone won't generate themes.

**Q: How do I invite someone to my team?**
A: Teams → your team → Settings → **Invite member**. They accept the email link while logged in.

**Q: What is SAML SSO?**
A: Single sign-on through your company identity provider (Chorus plan). Configure under Team settings → Authentication.

**Q: I didn't receive my magic link email — what now?**
A: Check spam, wait a few minutes, confirm the email address, and request a new link. Or sign in with password if you have one.

**Q: Can I delete my account or session data?**
A: Email support@qesto.cc for account deletion. Hosts can delete DRAFT sessions from the dashboard. Closed session data is retained up to 1 year unless deleted earlier.

**Q: Does Qesto work with Zoom, Teams, or Meet?**
A: Qesto handles polls and Q&A; pair it with your video call for audio/video. Share the Qesto link in the meeting chat.

**Q: Can multiple people host the same session?**
A: Add co-hosts to your team with **Launch session** permission. Only one presenter controls the live flow at a time.

Have more questions? Contact support@qesto.cc

---

title: Frequently Asked Questions: Technical Troubleshooting
excerpt: You've hit your plan's participant cap.
content:
## Technical Troubleshooting

### Connection Issues

**Problem: "Connection lost" message**

1. Check your internet connection
2. Refresh the page (F5)
3. Try a different browser
4. Disable VPN/proxy if using one
5. Clear browser cache (Ctrl+Shift+Delete)

**Problem: Session link doesn't work**

1. Make sure the session is actually started (not in DRAFT)
2. Try the link in an incognito/private window
3. Check the URL is correct
4. Ask the host to restart the session

### Participant Issues

**Problem: "Participant limit reached"**

You've hit your plan's participant cap. Either:
- Upgrade to a higher plan
- Create a separate session for additional participants

**Problem: Responses not showing**

1. Refresh the page
2. Make sure you submitted your response (look for confirmation)
3. Check that the question is still open

title: Frequently Asked Questions: Performance
excerpt: Qesto doesn't include audio/video.
content:
## Performance

**Problem: Session is lagging**

1. Close other browser tabs
2. Check your internet speed (broadband recommended)
3. If 500+ participants: wait for responses to settle

**Problem: Audio/video not working**

Qesto doesn't include audio/video. For that, use Zoom, Teams, or Google Meet separately.

title: Frequently Asked Questions: How to Use Energizers
excerpt: Energizers are fun, low-pressure warm-up activities that run before your main questions.
content:
## How to Use Energizers

### What Are Energizers?

Energizers are fun, low-pressure warm-up activities that run before your main questions. They help:

- Break the ice
- Test connection/device functionality
- Get everyone engaged
- Set a fun tone

### Available Energizers

#### Quick Finger

A speed game: See who can tap fastest.
- Participants tap as fast as possible for 10 seconds
- Leaderboard shows top tappers
- Great for remote teams to warm up

#### Team Quiz

A fun trivia game with instant scoring.
- Qesto gives you 3 fun team-focused questions
- Participants answer quickly
- Points awarded for speed + accuracy
- Leaderboard at the end

#### Word Cloud

Visual brainstorm: Type a word, watch a cloud form.
- Participants submit a single word
- Word size reflects frequency
- Fun way to see what's on everyone's mind
- Great for kickoff/retrospectives

### Adding Energizers

1. **In DRAFT mode**: Click "Add Energizer"
2. **Choose type**: Quick Finger, Team Quiz, or Word Cloud
3. **Configure** (if needed): Set time limits, questions
4. **Save**: Energizer is now in your session

### Running Energizers

1. **Start your session**: Energizers activate automatically
2. **Let it run**: Default is 1-2 minutes per energizer
3. **Watch live**: See responses flood in
4. **Auto-advance**: Or click "Next" manually

### Tips

- Use 1-2 energizers max (too many kills momentum)
- Great for sessions 30+ people
- Optional—skip them if your group knows each other
- Best for remote sessions (less need for in-person icebreakers)

Energizers make sessions more engaging and fun!

---

title: Frequently Asked Questions: Mobile and Accessibility Features
excerpt: Qesto is fully responsive and works on all devices:
content:
## Mobile and Accessibility Features

### Mobile Support

Qesto is fully responsive and works on all devices:

#### Phones
- Portrait and landscape orientation
- Touch-optimized buttons and inputs
- Fast loading on 4G/5G

#### Tablets
- Full-width layout for better readability
- Touch and stylus support
- Landscape recommended for presentations

#### Desktop
- Optimized for 1024px+ screens
- Works great on dual monitors

### Accessibility

Qesto meets WCAG 2.1 AA standards:

#### Keyboard Navigation
- Tab through all elements
- Enter to submit responses
- Escape to close modals
- Arrow keys for navigation

#### Screen Readers
- All buttons and links have labels
- Form fields are properly associated
- Live region announcements for updates

#### Color & Contrast
- High contrast colors (WCAG AA+)
- No color-only information
- Dark mode available

#### Text & Font
- Resizable text (Ctrl+ or Cmd+)
- Sans-serif font for readability
- 16px minimum font size

### Accessibility Settings

1. Click your profile → "Accessibility"
2. Options:
   - **Reduce motion**: Disable animations
   - **High contrast**: Increase color contrast
   - **Large text**: Increase default font size
   - **Dark mode**: Easy on the eyes
